You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw — roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation — but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the write scope and shared `references/`, keep changes semantic, and leave no stray scratch files. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving — the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references — The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml — how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.archon-horizon/runs/0013/sessions/0032-horizon-T9

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Task focus
Task: T9
Title: Jacobian⇄Albanese endgame glue
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=AlgebraicJacobian.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/*.lean,blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese*.tex,blueprint/src/chapters/Jacobian.tex

$$\forall\, (A,a)\ \text{pointed abelian variety},\quad \mathrm{Hom}_{\ast}(C,A)\ \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\ \mathrm{Hom}_{\mathrm{AbVar}}(J,A),$$
and is the unique such object up to canonical isomorphism.

You may read the Albanese subproject to see whether there has already been progress not updated with this project.

$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC — axiom-clean. The engine cone is sorry-free on Lean v4.31 (the former migration-interim sorries closed by T8). Residual base-change sorries live in `AJC.fbc`, not here.

$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). **02KG affine-reduction heart closed**: the per-$\sigma$ heart `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` (ring-pushout carve + corner-sections tensor rewrite + the affine brick `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`), together with `openImmersion_beckChevalley`/`bareBC`, the affine essential-image node (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`), the RHS tilde leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde`, and arbitrary-morphism pullback quasi-coherence (`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`, 01BG) are sorry-free and axiom-clean. $$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

# Opened inbox
- I-0118 issue (by=horizon, to=horizon, labels=agent-ready, projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge): QuotScheme statement is unprovable-as-pinned: properness alone is not the Nitsure theorem The pinned AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme (QuotFunctorDef.lean, final theorem) hypothesizes only [IsProper pi] [LocallyOfFiniteType pi] with arbitrary quasi-coherent L and arbitrary E, but the source theorem ([Nitsure] Sec. 5, thm:quot_representable) requires pi projective, L relatively very ample, E coherent. For merely proper X the Quot/Hilbert functor is in general only an algebraic space (Hironaka's smooth proper non-projective 3-fold is the standard counterexample family), so the pinned statement is false-or-open, not the cited theorem. Same trap genus as the old Grassmannian.representable sk…

# Memory
- **tangentSpaceIso (A.3.iii) is NOT directly provable — critical path runs through the relative Pic functor, and a naive FGA rewire is a FALSE-axiom trap.**

- `Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (`Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`) cannot be proven against the current foundation: `picSharp` in `FGAPicRepresentability.lean` is `Classical.choice` of a `⟨sorry⟩` `Nonempty` — a fully opaque functor. No H¹ comparison can attach to it. Do not re-probe the sorry directly.
- **Critical path** (in order): (1) ~~real relative Pic presheaf~~ DONE session 0015 (`PicSharp.relPresheaf` in `RelPicFunctor.lean`, `H_T`-coset carrier, axiom-clean); (2) étale sheafification target — Mathlib v4.31 has NO étale Grothendieck topology on schemes; either parametrize or use Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5: for C/k with a **k-rational section**, `Pic^♯(T) → Pic^♯_ét(T)` is already bijective, so representability can be stated against `relPresheaf` directly under a section hypothesis; (3) rewire FGA `picSharp` := (sheafified) relative functor — universe bump `Type u → Type (u+1)` in `HasPicSharp`/`HasPicScheme` is viable (`Functor.RepresentableBy` is universe-polymorphic; `Equiv` is cross-universe); (4) `Pic(C_ε) ≅ H¹(C_ε, O^×)` cocycle classification + truncated exponential split sequence `0 → O_C → O^×_{C_ε} → O^×_C → 1` for the kernel = `H¹(C,O_C)` computation (Kleiman Thm 5.11) — none of this exists yet, largest remaining block.
- **TRAP**: wiring `picSharp := PicSharp.presheaf` (the absolute functor, iso-class carrier) would make `PicSharpRepresentable`'s sorry a mathematically FALSE statement (absolute `Pic(C×T)` is not representable — it differs from `Hom(T, Pic)` by `Pic(T)`). Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4′ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try — no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.
- Milne 3.1/3.2 extension chain: "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension to a complete target" is FALSE — never re-add it; the honest chain and the reusable Mathlib extension recipe.

- **Do not reintroduce** an `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style statement (codim-1-free rational map to a complete variety extends): counterexample $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ away from a point (indeterminacy = one codim-2 point, no regular extension; blow-up needed). Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-≥2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- **Honest chain** (all in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` + `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`): Milne 3.1 = `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (proved, valuative); Milne 3.3 (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, the only remaining sorry) must assert the codim-1 witness lies **in $Z(f)$** (without that conjunct the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force emptiness); 3.3 + 3.1 ⇒ $Z(f)=\emptyset$ (`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`); then extension. **Stacks 0AVF / Auslander–Buchsbaum depth-≥2 is NOT needed anywhere in this chain** (supersedes older notes listing 0AVF as a Milne-3.1 blocker).
- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ⟨⊤, …⟩` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_ι` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg — the parser rejects `(ι := …)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict …).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).
- **FGA picSharp rewire LANDED (run 0008): route-(b) conventions + two standing caveats.**

State after the rewire (`Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`, **4 real `sorry`s**, all TRUE statements — Ground-verified run 0008 reconcile):

- `picSharp C := PicSharp.relPresheaf C ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat` (`Type (u+1)`-valued, REAL). `Scheme.HasRationalPoint C` = Nonempty section subtype of `C.hom`; the ONE genuine FGA sorry is `instHasPicScheme [HasRationalPoint C]`. The other three are typed-sorry carriers: `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` (Kleiman §4 `th:main`(1), true under the rational point) and the pre-existing Quot-blocked `HasDivFunctor` / `HasAbelMap`.

**Caveat 1 — pointless curves:** `JacobianWitness` (`Jacobian.lean`) requires the abelian variety $J$ to exist UNconditionally, but route (b) only builds `PicScheme` for pointed $C$. For a genus-$\ge 1$ curve without a rational point the eventual `picardJacobianWitness` proof needs Galois/étale descent from a finite extension where $C$ gains a point (or a pointed restatement of the north star). Not blocking anything now — record before anyone tries to close `picardJacobianWitness` directly.

**Caveat 2 — `smoothProperQuotient` statement is WEAKER than Kleiman lm:qt and unprovable as stated:** Mathlib v4.31 has NO quasi-projectivity vocabulary, so hypothesis (2) of lm:qt is missing from the Lean statement; without it the statement is FALSE (Hironaka-type free ℤ/2 action on a smooth proper non-projective 3-fold: smooth proper equivalence relation, étale-quotient sheaf a non-scheme algebraic space). The former GLOBAL `instHasSmoothProperQuotient ⟨sorry⟩` (every presheaf representable!) was deleted this run (Ground-confirmed gone); the class is a use-site hypothesis only. Do NOT reintroduce a global instance, and do NOT try to prove the theorem as stated.
- **`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (FBC leaf, `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:186`) is NOT a cheap adjointness win — Ground's recommendation had the variance backwards. Route analysis (session 0013), do not re-derive.**

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" — **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.
- No dodge exists at the presheaf level: `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is a bare `leftAdjoint` with no pointwise description; the sections-level formula `colim_{U ⊇ g(U')} M(U) ⊗_{O(U)} O(U')` is only RIGHT-exact (the ring maps are not flat — flatness lives on stalks). Sheafification + `SheafOfModules.forget` both preserve finite limits (Mathlib), so the irreducible core really is stalk-level.
- **Viable honest route** (est. 400–800 LOC of new infra): (1) combinators exist — `Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_tfae` / `preservesFiniteLimits_of_preservesKernels` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/ShortComplex/ExactFunctor.lean`); (2) Mathlib 2026 has module structure on stalks (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Stalk.lean`, `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`) and stalk⊣skyscraper at plain-category level (`Topology/Sheaves/Skyscraper.lean`), plus `mono_iff_stalk_mono`/`isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (`Topology/Sheaves/Stalks.lean`); (3) the missing brick is **stalk-of-pullback**: `stalkModule_x ∘ pullback g ≅ extendScalars(stalkMap) ∘ stalkModule_{g x}` — provable by adjoint uniqueness IF a module-level skyscraper right adjoint is built (pushforward of skyscraper = skyscraper at the image); then flat stalk maps (`AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.iff_flat_stalkMap`-adjacent, `RingHom.Flat` appLE definition) + `ModuleCat.preservesFiniteLimits_extendScalars_of_flat` (`ModuleCat/Descent.lean`) finish via kernel-comparison-on-stalks.
- **Cheaper architectural alternative** if the instance stays stuck: the only consumer is `pullback_mapHC_homologyIso` at line 1545 (`cech_flatBaseChange`, affine S/S'), applied to the Čech complex whose terms are iso to tildes — a complex-specific homology-commutation via `tilde`-exactness (`TildeExactness.lean`) + `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` could replace the general instance, at the cost of a termwise-tilde chain-iso.
- **A.3 Pic0 cone state after run-0008 T5 — supersedes the run-0005 'IdentityComponent all 9 sorries blocked' memory.**

- **Proved sorry-free**: `Pic0.grpObj` (`Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C))` = `isSubgroupHomomorphism (PicScheme C)`; instances synthesize directly from `groupSchemeStructure` (CommGrpObj parent projection) + the `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` projection instance; `Pic0Scheme` unfolds by rfl, plain `exact` works). Also `Pic0.geometricallyIrreducible` (specialisation of the substrate), both `isAbelianVariety` assemblies (the `Pic0Scheme`-namespace one MOVED to `Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`), and `tangentSpaceCotangentDual` now hypothesis-free.

Chevalley–Rosenlicht).

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Precomposition of a rational map with an open morphism
10	
11	Mathlib's `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap` API provides only `compHom`,
12	the composition of a rational map with a *morphism on the right*
13	(`f : X ⤏ Y`, `g : Y ⟶ Z` ↦ `X ⤏ Z`). The dual operation — precomposing a
14	rational map with a morphism *on the left* — is missing.
15	
16	For a morphism `p : W ⟶ X` whose underlying continuous map is **open**, and a
17	rational map `f : X ⤏ Y`, the composite `f ∘ p : W ⤏ Y` is a well-defined
18	rational map. Openness of `p` is exactly what guarantees that the preimage
19	`p⁻¹(f.domain)` of the (dense open) domain of definition stays dense
20	(`Dense.preimage`), so that the pulled-back partial map is again a partial map,
21	and that the construction descends to equivalence classes.
22	
23	This is the first missing brick in Milne's difference-map construction
24	`Φ = m ∘ (f × f) : X × X ⤏ G` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Lemma 3.3): the two
25	projections `prᵢ : X × X ⟶ X` are open (flat, locally of finite presentation,
26	being base changes of the smooth structure morphism `X ⟶ Spec k̄`), so
27	`f.precomp prᵢ` is the pullback of `f` along a projection.
28	
29	## Main definitions
30	
31	* `Scheme.PartialMap.precomp` — precomposition of a partial map with an open
32	  morphism.
33	* `Scheme.RationalMap.precomp` — precomposition of a rational map with an open
34	  morphism, descended from `PartialMap.precomp`.
35	
36	## Main results
37	
38	* `Scheme.RationalMap.precomp_compHom` — precomposition commutes with
39	  right-composition, `(f.precomp p hp).compHom g = (f.compHom g).precomp p hp`.
40	-/
41	
42	set_option autoImplicit false
43	
44	universe u
45	
46	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace
47	
48	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
49	
50	namespace Scheme
51	
52	variable {W X Y Z : Scheme.{u}}
53	
54	namespace PartialMap
55	
56	/-- **Auxiliary restriction square.** For a morphism `p : W ⟶ X` and opens
57	`V ≤ U` of `X`, restricting `p ∣_ U` to the smaller preimage `p ⁻¹ᵁ V` agrees
58	with `p ∣_ V` followed by the inclusion `V ↪ U`. Both sides are pinned down by
59	composing with the mono `U.ι`. -/
60	lemma homOfLE_comp_morphismRestrict (p : W ⟶ X) {U V : X.Opens} (hUV : V ≤ U)
61	    (h : p ⁻¹ᵁ V ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
62	    W.homOfLE h ≫ (p ∣_ U) = (p ∣_ V) ≫ X.homOfLE hUV := by
63	  rw [← cancel_mono U.ι, Category.assoc, morphismRestrict_ι,
64	    ← Category.assoc (W.homOfLE h), Scheme.homOfLE_ι, Category.assoc,
65	    Scheme.homOfLE_ι, morphismRestrict_ι]
66	
67	/-- **Precomposition of a partial map with an open morphism.** For `f : X ⤏ Y`
68	(as a partial map) and a morphism `p : W ⟶ X` whose underlying map is open, the
69	composite `f ∘ p : W ⤏ Y` is defined on `p⁻¹(f.domain)`, which is dense because
70	open maps pull dense sets back to dense sets. -/
71	@[simps domain hom]
72	noncomputable def precomp (f : X.PartialMap Y) (p : W ⟶ X) (hp : IsOpenMap p.base) :
73	    W.PartialMap Y where
74	  domain := p ⁻¹ᵁ f.domain
75	  dense_domain := f.dense_domain.preimage hp
76	  hom := (p ∣_ f.domain) ≫ f.hom
77	
78	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
79	/-- Precomposition respects the equivalence of partial maps, hence descends to
80	rational maps. -/
81	lemma precomp_equiv {f g : X.PartialMap Y} (p : W ⟶ X) (hp : IsOpenMap p.base)
82	    (h : f.equiv g) : (f.precomp p hp).equiv (g.precomp p hp) := by
83	  obtain ⟨V, hV, hVl, hVr, e⟩ := h
84	  have hVd : Dense ((p ⁻¹ᵁ V : W.Opens) : Set W) := by
85	    rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.map_coe]; exact hV.preimage hp
86	  refine ⟨p ⁻¹ᵁ V, hVd, p.preimage_mono hVl, p.preimage_mono hVr, ?_⟩
87	  simp only [restrict_hom, precomp_hom] at e ⊢
88	  rw [← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc,
89	    homOfLE_comp_morphismRestrict p hVl, homOfLE_comp_morphismRestrict p hVr,
90	    Category.assoc, Category.assoc, e]
91	
92	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
93	/-- Precomposition with an `S`-morphism preserves being an `S`-map: if `f` is an
94	`S`-rational map and `p : W ⟶ X` is an `S`-morphism, then `f ∘ p` is an
95	`S`-rational map. In Milne's setting all schemes live over `k̄`, so the difference
96	map is automatically a `k̄`-map. -/
97	instance precomp_isOver {S : Scheme.{u}} [W.Over S] [X.Over S] [Y.Over S]
98	    (f : X.PartialMap Y) [f.IsOver S] (p : W ⟶ X) [p.IsOver S] (hp : IsOpenMap p.base) :
99	    (f.precomp p hp).IsOver S := by
100	  haveI hrestr : (p ∣_ f.domain).IsOver S := by
101	    rw [Hom.isOver_iff]
102	    change (p ∣_ f.domain) ≫ (f.domain.ι ≫ X ↘ S) = (p ⁻¹ᵁ f.domain).ι ≫ W ↘ S
103	    rw [← Category.assoc, morphismRestrict_ι, Category.assoc, comp_over]
104	  change ((p ∣_ f.domain) ≫ f.hom).IsOver S
105	  infer_instance
106	
107	end PartialMap
108	
109	/-- **Precomposition of a rational map with an open morphism.** For `f : X ⤏ Y`
110	and a morphism `p : W ⟶ X` whose underlying map is open, `f.precomp p hp : W ⤏ Y`
111	is the composite `f ∘ p`, obtained by pulling any representative partial map back
112	along `p`. -/
113	noncomputable def RationalMap.precomp (f : X ⤏ Y) (p : W ⟶ X) (hp : IsOpenMap p.base) :
114	    W ⤏ Y :=
115	  Quotient.map (PartialMap.precomp · p hp) (fun _ _ ↦ PartialMap.precomp_equiv p hp) f
116	
117	@[simp]
118	lemma RationalMap.precomp_toRationalMap (f : X.PartialMap Y) (p : W ⟶ X)
119	    (hp : IsOpenMap p.base) :
120	    (f.precomp p hp).toRationalMap = f.toRationalMap.precomp p hp := rfl
121	
122	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
123	/-- Precomposition on the left commutes with right-composition by a morphism.
124	This is what lets Milne's difference map be assembled as
125	`(f.precomp prᵢ).compHom (m ∘ (id × inv))`. -/
126	lemma RationalMap.precomp_compHom (f : X ⤏ Y) (p : W ⟶ X) (hp : IsOpenMap p.base)
127	    (g : Y ⟶ Z) :
128	    (f.precomp p hp).compHom g = (f.compHom g).precomp p hp := by
129	  obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := f.exists_rep
130	  simp only [← RationalMap.precomp_toRationalMap, ← RationalMap.compHom_toRationalMap]
131	  refine congrArg PartialMap.toRationalMap (PartialMap.ext _ _ rfl ?_)
132	  rw [Scheme.isoOfEq_rfl, Iso.refl_hom, Category.id_comp,
133	    PartialMap.compHom_hom, PartialMap.precomp_hom, PartialMap.precomp_hom,
134	    PartialMap.compHom_hom, Category.assoc]
135	  rfl
136	
137	end Scheme
138	
139	end AlgebraicGeometry
140

1600	`CodimOneFree`), yet admits no regular extension (any extension `g` would
1601	force `⊤ = g.toPartialMap.domain ≤ f.domain` via
1602	`PartialMap.le_domain_toRationalMap`, but `f.domain = ℙ² ∖ {pt}`). Milne 3.1
1603	asserts only the codim-≥2 conclusion; the everywhere-extension in Milne 3.2
1604	needs the *group-variety* input of Lemma 3.3 to force `Z(f) = ∅` first.
1605	
1606	Blueprint reference: `thm:codim_one_extension`. -/
1607	theorem existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty
1608	    {X Y : Scheme.{u}} [IsReduced X] [Y.IsSeparated]
1609	    (f : X.RationalMap Y) (h : indeterminacyLocus f = ∅) :
1610	    ∃! (g : X ⟶ Y), g.toRationalMap = f := by
1611	  -- The domain of definition is all of `X`.
1612	  have hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤ := by
1613	    rw [toPartialMap_domain, ← TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_inj,
1614	      TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_top, ← Set.compl_empty_iff]
1615	    exact h
1616	  -- The candidate morphism: transport the maximal representative along
1617	  -- `X ≅ ⊤ ≅ f.toPartialMap.domain`.
1618	  have hwit : (X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge
1619	      ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom).toRationalMap = f := by
1620	    conv_rhs => rw [← f.toRationalMap_toPartialMap]
1621	    refine PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ?_
1622	    refine ⟨⊤, by rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_top]; exact dense_univ,
1623	      le_top, hdom.ge, ?_⟩
1624	    simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap,
1625	      Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc,
1626	      Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]
1627	  exact ⟨_, hwit, fun g hg => hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq (hg.trans hwit.symm)⟩
1628	
1629	/-! ## §5. Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure-codim-1 indeterminacy into a group variety
1630	
1631	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety has its
1632	indeterminacy locus either empty or of pure codimension `1`. Combined with
1633	`thm:indeterminacy_codimGe2`'s codim-≥2 conclusion, this forces the locus
1634	to be empty when the target is an abelian variety, at which point
1635	`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty` extends.
1636	
1637	Blueprint pin: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Lemma 3.3 p. 17). -/
1638	
1639	/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3 — indeterminacy into a group variety is empty or pure
1640	codim 1.**
1641	
1642	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
1643	(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
1644	rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
1645	either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or of *pure codimension 1*,
1646	encoded as: every point of `Z(f)` lies in the closure of a codim-1 point
1647	*of `Z(f)` itself*. (The `z ∈ indeterminacyLocus f` conjunct is essential:
1648	without it the disjunct is nearly vacuous — on a variety every non-generic
1649	point specialises from *some* codim-1 point — and, in particular, too weak
1650	to combine with the codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1 to force `Z(f) = ∅`
1651	in Theorem 3.2. Statement strengthened in run-0006 T6, session 0015.)
1652	
1653	The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
1654	`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
1655	iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
1656	a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
1657	codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
1658	locus of `f` as pure codim-1.
1659	
1660	Blueprint reference: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
1661	Varieties*, Lemma 3.3, §I.3, p. 17). -/
1662	theorem indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme
1663	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]

data.content:
1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Function-field pullback of a rational map
10	
11	For a rational map `f : X ⤏ Y` out of an integral scheme `X`, Mathlib's
12	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.fromFunctionField` gives a morphism
13	`Spec K(X) ⟶ Y` (the restriction of `f` to the generic point). Composing it with
14	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo` produces the pullback of germs
15	`𝒪_{Y, f(η_X)} ⟶ K(X)` at the image of the generic point of `X`.
16	
17	When `f` is **dominant** and `Y` is irreducible, that image point is the generic
18	point of `Y`, so the germ pullback becomes a field homomorphism
19	`K(Y) ⟶ K(X)` — the function-field functoriality `K(Y) → K(X)` that Milne's
20	rational-map extension leg (`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`) and the
21	Weil-divisor obstruction (`thm:weil_divisor_obstruction`) both require.
22	
23	## Main definitions
24	
25	* `Scheme.RationalMap.stalkPullback` — the germ pullback
26	  `𝒪_{Y, f(η_X)} ⟶ K(X)` (no dominance needed).
27	* `Scheme.RationalMap.fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint` — for a dominant
28	  rational map into an irreducible target, `f(η_X) = η_Y`.
29	* `Scheme.RationalMap.functionFieldPullback` — the induced field homomorphism
30	  `K(Y) ⟶ K(X)` for a dominant rational map.
31	-/
32	
33	set_option autoImplicit false
34	
35	universe u
36	
37	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace IsLocalRing
38	
39	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
40	
41	namespace Scheme
42	
43	namespace RationalMap
44	
45	variable {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
46	
47	/-- **Germ pullback along a rational map.** For a rational map `f : X ⤏ Y` out of
48	an integral scheme `X`, the associated morphism `f.fromFunctionField : Spec K(X) ⟶ Y`
49	induces, by `Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo`, a local ring homomorphism from the germ
50	of `Y` at the image `f(η_X)` of the generic point of `X` into the function field
51	`K(X)`. -/
52	noncomputable def stalkPullback [IsIntegral X] (f : X.RationalMap Y) :
53	    Y.presheaf.stalk (f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint X.functionField)) ⟶ X.functionField :=
54	  Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo f.fromFunctionField
55	
56	/-!
57	## Dominant refinement
58	
59	When `f` is **dominant** and `Y` is irreducible, the base point
60	`f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint K(X))` equals `genericPoint Y`, so `stalkPullback`
61	becomes a field homomorphism `K(Y) ⟶ K(X)`.
62	-/
63	
64	/-- **For a dominant rational map into an irreducible target, the function-field
65	morphism sends the closed point of `Spec K(X)` to the generic point of `Y`.**
66	
67	Reducing to a representative partial map `g`, the morphism
68	`g.fromFunctionField = g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (η_X) ≫ g.hom` sends the closed
69	point of `Spec 𝒪_{X, η_X} = Spec K(X)` to `g.hom (η_{g.domain})`. The inner point
70	is the generic point of the domain (checked after applying the open immersion
71	`g.domain.ι`, using `Opens.fromSpecStalkOfMem_ι` and `fromSpecStalk_closedPoint`),
72	and a dominant morphism sends the generic point of its (irreducible) source to the
73	generic point of `Y` (image of a generic point is generic on the dense range, and
74	generic points are unique in the T₀ space `Y`). -/
75	theorem fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint [IsIntegral X] [IrreducibleSpace Y]
76	    (f : X.RationalMap Y) [f.IsDominant] :
77	    f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint X.functionField) = genericPoint Y := by
78	  obtain ⟨g, rfl⟩ := f.exists_rep
79	  haveI : IsDominant g.hom := (PartialMap.isDominant_toRationalMap_iff g).mp ‹_›
80	  have hη : genericPoint X ∈ g.domain :=
81	    (genericPoint_specializes _).mem_open g.domain.2 g.dense_domain.nonempty.choose_spec
82	  haveI : Nonempty (g.domain : Type _) := ⟨⟨genericPoint X, hη⟩⟩
83	  haveI : IsIntegral g.domain.toScheme := inferInstance
84	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace g.domain.toScheme := inferInstance
85	  -- reduce the rational-map `fromFunctionField` to the partial-map one, then to the
86	  -- composite `g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (η_X) hη ≫ g.hom`
87	  rw [RationalMap.fromFunctionField_toRationalMap]
88	  have hcomp : PartialMap.fromFunctionField g
89	      = g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (genericPoint X) hη ≫ g.hom := rfl
90	  rw [hcomp, Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
91	  -- the inner point is the generic point of the domain
92	  have hp : (g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (genericPoint X) hη) (closedPoint X.functionField)
93	      = genericPoint g.domain.toScheme := by
94	    apply (Scheme.Opens.ι g.domain).isOpenEmbedding.injective
95	    rw [genericPoint_eq_of_isOpenImmersion (Scheme.Opens.ι g.domain),
96	      ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, Opens.fromSpecStalkOfMem_ι]
97	    exact Scheme.fromSpecStalk_closedPoint
98	  rw [hp]
99	  -- a dominant morphism sends the generic point of an irreducible source to `genericPoint Y`
100	  have himg := (genericPoint_spec g.domain.toScheme).image g.hom.continuous
101	  rw [Set.image_univ, g.hom.denseRange.closure_range] at himg
102	  exact himg.eq (genericPoint_spec Y)
103	
104	/-- **The induced field homomorphism `K(Y) ⟶ K(X)` of a dominant rational map.**
105	
106	Since `f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint K(X)) = genericPoint Y`
107	(`fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint`), the germ pullback `stalkPullback` has
108	source `𝒪_{Y, η_Y} = K(Y)` up to the canonical stalk isomorphism, giving the
109	function-field functoriality `K(Y) → K(X)` required by the rational-map extension
110	leg of Milne's Albanese argument. -/
111	noncomputable def functionFieldPullback [IsIntegral X] [IrreducibleSpace Y]
112	    (f : X.RationalMap Y) [f.IsDominant] :
113	    Y.functionField ⟶ X.functionField :=
114	  (Y.presheaf.stalkCongr
115	    (Inseparable.of_eq (fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint f).symm)).hom ≫ f.stalkPullback
116	
117	end RationalMap
118	
119	end Scheme
120	
121	end AlgebraicGeometry

43	/--
44	A partial map from `X` to `Y` (`X.PartialMap Y`) is a morphism into `Y`
45	defined on a dense open subscheme of `X`.
46	-/
47	structure PartialMap (X Y : Scheme.{u}) where
48	  /-- The domain of definition of a partial map. -/
49	  domain : X.Opens
50	  dense_domain : Dense (domain : Set X)
51	  /-- The underlying morphism of a partial map. -/
52	  hom : ↑domain ⟶ Y
53	
54	variable (S) in
55	/-- A partial map is an `S`-map if the underlying morphism is. -/
56	abbrev PartialMap.IsOver [X.Over S] [Y.Over S] (f : X.PartialMap Y) :=
57	  f.hom.IsOver S
58	
59	namespace PartialMap
60	
61	lemma ext_iff (f g : X.PartialMap Y) :
62	    f = g ↔ ∃ e : f.domain = g.domain, f.hom = (X.isoOfEq e).hom ≫ g.hom := by
63	  constructor
64	  · rintro rfl
65	    simp
66	  · obtain ⟨U, hU, f⟩ := f
67	    obtain ⟨V, hV, g⟩ := g
68	    rintro ⟨rfl : U = V, e⟩
69	    congr 1
70	    simpa using e
71	
72	@[ext]
73	lemma ext (f g : X.PartialMap Y) (e : f.domain = g.domain)
74	    (H : f.hom = (X.isoOfEq e).hom ≫ g.hom) : f = g := by
75	  rw [ext_iff]
76	  exact ⟨e, H⟩
77	
78	/-- The restriction of a partial map to a smaller domain. -/
79	@[simps hom domain]
80	noncomputable
81	def restrict (f : X.PartialMap Y) (U : X.Opens)
82	    (hU : Dense (U : Set X)) (hU' : U ≤ f.domain) : X.PartialMap Y where
83	  domain := U
84	  dense_domain := hU
85	  hom := X.homOfLE hU' ≫ f.hom

87	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
88	@[simp]

90	  ext1 <;> simp [restrict_domain]

93	    (f.restrict f.domain f.dense_domain le_rfl).hom = f.hom := by

1724	The difference map of Milne's Lemma~3.3 (\cref{sec:milne_lem33} below) is built by
1725	precomposing \(f\) with the two projections \(X \times X \to X\) and pairing the results.
1726	The precomposition operation is the following; it is the left-hand dual of the
1727	right-composition \(\mathrm{compHom}\) already provided by the rational-map API.
1728	
1729	\begin{definition}\leanok
1730	  \label{def:rationalMap_precomp}
1731	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.precomp}
1732	  Let \(p \colon W \to X\) be a morphism of schemes whose underlying continuous map is
1733	  \emph{open}, and let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map. The
1734	  \textbf{precomposition} \(f \circ p \colon W \dashrightarrow Y\) is the rational map
1735	  represented, for any partial-map representative \((V, \varphi_V)\) of \(f\), by
1736	  \[
1737	    \bigl(p^{-1}(V),\; \; p^{-1}(V) \xrightarrow{\;p|_{p^{-1}(V)}\;} V
1738	    \xrightarrow{\;\varphi_V\;} Y\bigr).
1739	  \]
1740	  The domain \(p^{-1}(V)\) is dense because the preimage of a dense open set under an open
1741	  continuous map is dense; the same fact makes the construction independent of the chosen
1742	  representative, so it descends to a well-defined rational map.
1743	\end{definition}
1744	
1745	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1746	  \label{lem:rationalMap_precomp_compHom}
1747	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.precomp_compHom}
1748	  \uses{def:rationalMap_precomp}
1749	  Precomposition on the left commutes with right-composition by a morphism: for
1750	  \(p \colon W \to X\) open, \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\), and \(g \colon Y \to Z\),
1751	  \[
1752	    (f \circ p) \circ g \;=\; (f \circ g) \circ p
1753	    \qquad\text{in}\qquad W \dashrightarrow Z .
1754	  \]
1755	  Furthermore, if \(p\) and \(f\) are defined over a base scheme \(S\), then so is
1756	  \(f \circ p\).
1757	\end{lemma}
1758	\begin{proof}\leanok
1759	  Both sides act on a chosen partial-map representative \((V, \varphi_V)\) of \(f\) by
1760	  restriction along \(p\) and composition with \(g\); the resulting equality is the
1761	  associativity of morphism composition,
1762	  \((p|_{p^{-1}(V)} \circ \varphi_V) \circ g = p|_{p^{-1}(V)} \circ (\varphi_V \circ g)\).
1763	  For the over-\(S\) claim, \(p|_{p^{-1}(V)}\) is an \(S\)-morphism because it commutes with
1764	  the structure morphisms via the restriction identity
1765	  \(p|_{p^{-1}(V)} \circ (V \hookrightarrow X) = (p^{-1}(V) \hookrightarrow W) \circ p\),
1766	  and a composite of \(S\)-morphisms is an \(S\)-morphism.
1767	\end{proof}

1938	  Following Milne's proof of Lemma~3.3. The argument has four sub-steps. Throughout we
1939	  fix a representative \((U, \varphi_U)\) of \(f\).
1940	
1941	  \emph{Sub-step 1: the difference rational map.} Define
1942	  \[
1943	    \Phi \colon X \times X \dashrightarrow G,
1944	    \qquad
1945	    \Phi(x, y) \;:=\; \varphi(x) \cdot \varphi(y)^{-1},
1946	  \]
1947	  via the composition
1948	  \[
1949	    U \times U
1950	    \;\xrightarrow{\varphi_U \times \varphi_U}\; G \times G
1951	    \;\xrightarrow{\mathrm{id} \times \mathrm{inv}}\; G \times G
1952	    \;\xrightarrow{m}\; G,
1953	  \]
1954	  where \(\mathrm{inv} \colon G \to G\) is the group-variety inverse and
1955	  \(m \colon G \times G \to G\) is the group law. The map \(\Phi\) is rational by
1956	  composition: precomposing \(f\) with each projection
1957	  \(\mathrm{pr}_i \colon X \times X \to X\) (which is open, being a base change of the
1958	  flat, locally-finitely-presented structure morphism \(X \to \Spec \bar k\)) yields
1959	  rational maps \(f \circ \mathrm{pr}_i \colon X \times X \dashrightarrow G\)
1960	  (\cref{def:rationalMap_precomp}, whose over-\(\bar k\) part is
1961	  \cref{lem:rationalMap_precomp_compHom}); pairing them into \(X \times X \dashrightarrow
1962	  G \times G\) and composing on the right with \((\mathrm{id} \times \mathrm{inv})\) then
1963	  \(m\) gives \(\Phi\). Its domain is at least \(U \times U \subseteq X \times X\), which
1964	  is dense because \(U\) is dense in \(X\).
1965	
1966	  \emph{Sub-step 2: \(\Phi\)-defined at \((x, x)\) \(\Leftrightarrow\) \(\varphi\)-defined at
1967	  \(x\).} If \(\varphi\) is defined at \(x\), the explicit formula \(\Phi(x, x) = e\) (the
1968	  identity of \(G\)) shows \(\Phi\) is defined at \((x, x)\). Conversely, if \(\Phi\) is
1969	  defined at \((x, x)\), openness of \(\mathrm{Dom}(\Phi)\) in \(X \times X\) produces an
1970	  open set \(W \ni (x, x)\) on which \(\Phi\) is defined; by projection (taking the slice

1972	  \(W'\) so \(\varphi\) is defined on \(W'\) as well --- \(W'\) need not contain \(x\)), the
1973	  identity

1975	    \varphi(x) \;=\; \Phi(x, u) \cdot \varphi(u),
1976	    \qquad u \in W',

2008	    \;=\; \bigl\{\, f \in K(X \times X) \;\big|\;
2009	    \mathrm{div}(f)_\infty \text{ does not contain } (x, x) \,\bigr\} \cup \{0\}.

/-

Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Christian Merten

import Mathlib

/-!
# Pairing of two rational maps into a fibre product

Mathlib's `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap` API provides `compHom`
(right-composition with a morphism) but no way to *pair* two rational maps out of
a common source into a fibre product. For Milne's difference-map construction
`Φ = m ∘ (f × f) : X × X ⤏ G` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Lemma 3.3) one needs
exactly this pairing: given `f ∘ pr₁` and `f ∘ pr₂ : X × X ⤏ G`, assemble the map
`X × X ⤏ G ×_{k̄} G` whose two projections recover the factors, so that it can be
right-composed with the group law `G ×_{k̄} G → G`.

The naive `PartialMap`-level pairing via `pullback.lift` does **not** descend to
rational maps through `Quotient.map₂`: `RationalMap.IsOver S` guarantees only *one*
over-`S` representative, not that an arbitrary pair of representatives is
simultaneously over `S`, so the compatibility hypothesis fails. Instead we use the
function-field correspondence: over an integral source `X`, a rational map
`X ⤏ Y` over `S` is the same datum as an `S`-morphism `Spec K(X) ⟶ Y`
(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.ofFunctionField` /
`equivFunctionField`), and morphisms out of the single scheme `Spec K(X)` pair
freely by `pullback.lift`.

To stay clear of `Over`-typeclass diamonds on the pullback target we work with
explicit structure morphisms `sX : X ⟶ S`, `sY : Y ⟶ S`, `sZ : Z ⟶ S` (matching
mathlib's `equivFunctionField`), and phrase the over-`S` datum as the equation
`a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap`. In Milne's setting `S = Spec k̄`,
`sX = X.hom`, `sY = sZ = G.hom`, and the target `pullback G.hom G.hom` is
definitionally `(G ⊗ G).left` (`CategoryTheory.Over.tensorObj_left`), so the group
law `(GrpObj.mul).left : (G ⊗ G).left ⟶ G.left` composes on the right directly.

## Main definitions

* `Scheme.RationalMap.prod` — the pairing `X ⤏ pullback sY sZ` of `a : X ⤏ Y` and
  `b : X ⤏ Z`, both over `S`.

## Main results

* `Scheme.RationalMap.fromFunctionField_compHom` — the function-field morphism of a
  right-composition is the composite of function-field morphisms.
* `Scheme.RationalMap.prod_compHom_fst` / `prod_compHom_snd` — the two projections of
  the pairing recover the original rational maps.
* `Scheme.RationalMap.prod_compHom_over` — the pairing is again over `S`.
-/

set_option autoImplicit false

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

namespace Scheme

namespace RationalMap

variable {X Y Z S : Scheme.{u}}

/-- **Function-field functoriality of right-composition.** For a rational map
`f : X ⤏ Y` out of an irreducible scheme and a morphism `g : Y ⟶ Z`, the associated
morphism `Spec K(X) ⟶ Z` of `f.compHom g` is `f.fromFunctionField ≫ g`. -/
@[simp]
lemma fromFunctionField_compHom [IrreducibleSpace X] (f : X ⤏ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) :
    (f.compHom g).fromFunctionField = f.fromFunctionField ≫ g := by
  obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := f.exists_rep
  rw [← compHom_toRationalMap, fromFunctionField_toRationalMap, fromFunctionField_toRationalMap]
  exact PartialMap.fromSpecStalkOfMem_compHom f g _ _

/-- The function-field morphism of an over-`S` rational map factors the structure
morphism: if `a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap`, then
`a.fromFunctionField ≫ sY = X.fromSpecStalk η ≫ sX`. -/
lemma fromFunctionField_comp_structure [IsIntegral X] (a : X ⤏ Y) (sX : X ⟶ S)
    (sY : Y ⟶ S) (ha : a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap) :
    a.fromFunctionField ≫ sY = X.fromSpecStalk (genericPoint X) ≫ sX := by
  rw [← fromFunctionField_compHom, ha, Scheme.Hom.toRationalMap, fromFunctionField_toRationalMap]
  exact PartialMap.fromSpecStalkOfMem_toPartialMap sX _

/-- **Pairing of two rational maps into a fibre product.** For rational maps
`a : X ⤏ Y` and `b : X ⤏ Z` out of an integral scheme `X`, both defined over `S`
(encoded as `a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap`, resp. for `b`), the pairing
`a.prod b : X ⤏ pullback sY sZ` is the rational map whose function-field morphism is
`pullback.lift a.fromFunctionField b.fromFunctionField`.

Milne's difference map is `(f.precomp pr₁).prod (f.precomp pr₂)` followed by
right-composition with the group law of `G`. -/
noncomputable def prod (sX : X ⟶ S) (sY : Y ⟶ S) (sZ : Z ⟶ S)
    [IsIntegral X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY] [LocallyOfFiniteType sZ]
    (a : X ⤏ Y) (b : X ⤏ Z)
    (ha : a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap) (hb : b.compHom sZ = sX.toRationalMap) :
    X ⤏ pullback sY sZ :=
  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType (pullback.fst sY sZ ≫ sY) :=
    MorphismProperty.comp_mem _ _ _ inferInstance inferInstance
  ofFunctionField sX (pullback.fst sY sZ ≫ sY)
    (pullback.lift a.fromFunctionField b.fromFunctionField
      ((fromFunctionField_comp_structure a sX sY ha).trans
        (fromFunctionField_comp_structure b sX sZ hb).symm))
    (by rw [pullback.lift_fst_assoc]; exact fromFunctionField_comp_structure a sX sY ha)

/-- The function-field morphism of the pairing is the `pullback.lift` of the factors'
function-field morphisms. -/
@[simp]
lemma prod_fromFunctionField (sX : X ⟶ S) (sY : Y ⟶ S) (sZ : Z ⟶ S)
    [IsIntegral X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY] [LocallyOfFiniteType sZ]
    (a : X ⤏ Y) (b : X ⤏ Z)
    (ha : a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap) (hb : b.compHom sZ = sX.toRationalMap) :
    (prod sX sY sZ a b ha hb).fromFunctionField
      = pullback.lift a.fromFunctionField b.fromFunctionField
        ((fromFunctionField_comp_structure a sX sY ha).trans
          (fromFunctionField_comp_structure b sX sZ hb).symm) := by
  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType (pullback.fst sY sZ ≫ sY) :=
    MorphismProperty.comp_mem _ _ _ inferInstance inferInstance
  exact fromFunctionField_ofFunctionField _ _ _ _

/-- **First projection of the pairing.** Right-composing the pairing with
`pullback.fst` recovers `a`. -/
lemma prod_compHom_fst (sX : X ⟶ S) (sY : Y ⟶ S) (sZ : Z ⟶ S)
    [IsIntegral X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY] [LocallyOfFiniteType sZ]
    (a : X ⤏ Y) (b : X ⤏ Z)
    (ha : a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap) (hb : b.compHom sZ = sX.toRationalMap) :
    (prod sX sY sZ a b ha hb).compHom (pullback.fst sY sZ) = a :=
  eq_of_fromFunctionField_eq _ _ (by
    rw [fromFunctionField_compHom, prod_fromFunctionField, pullback.lift_fst])

/-- **Second projection of the pairing.** Right-composing the pairing with
`pullback.snd` recovers `b`. -/
lemma prod_compHom_snd (sX : X ⟶ S) (sY : Y ⟶ S) (sZ : Z ⟶ S)
    [IsIntegral X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY] [LocallyOfFiniteType sZ]
    (a : X ⤏ Y) (b : X ⤏ Z)
    (ha : a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap) (hb : b.compHom sZ = sX.toRationalMap) :
    (prod sX sY sZ a b ha hb).compHom (pullback.snd sY sZ) = b :=
  eq_of_fromFunctionField_eq _ _ (by
    rw [fromFunctionField_compHom, prod_fromFunctionField, pullback.lift_snd])

/-- **The pairing is over `S`.** Right-composing with the structure morphism
`pullback.fst sY sZ ≫ sY` of the fibre product recovers `sX.toRationalMap`. -/
lemma prod_compHom_over (sX : X ⟶ S) (sY : Y ⟶ S) (sZ : Z ⟶ S)
    [IsIntegral X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY] [LocallyOfFiniteType sZ]
    (a : X ⤏ Y) (b : X ⤏ Z)
    (ha : a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap) (hb : b.compHom sZ = sX.toRationalMap) :
    (prod sX sY sZ a b ha hb).compHom (pullback.fst sY sZ ≫ sY) = sX.toRationalMap := by
  rw [← compHom_compHom, prod_compHom_fst, ha]

end RationalMap

end Scheme

end AlgebraicGeometry

\end{proof}

To pair the two precompositions \(f \circ \mathrm{pr}_1\) and \(f \circ \mathrm{pr}_2\) into
a single rational map \(X \times X \dashrightarrow G \times_{\bar k} G\), we use the
function-field correspondence: for an integral scheme \(X\) over \(S\) and a scheme \(Y\)
locally of finite type over \(S\), a rational map \(X \dashrightarrow Y\) over \(S\) is the
same datum as an \(S\)-morphism \(\operatorname{Spec} K(X) \to Y\) (restriction to the generic
point, spread out again by local finite type). Morphisms out of the single scheme
\(\operatorname{Spec} K(X)\) pair freely through the universal property of the fibre product.

\begin{definition}\leanok
  \label{def:rationalMap_prod}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.prod}
  Let \(s_X \colon X \to S\), \(s_Y \colon Y \to S\), \(s_Z \colon Z \to S\) be structure
  morphisms with \(X\) integral and \(s_Y, s_Z\) locally of finite type, and let
  \(a \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\), \(b \colon X \dashrightarrow Z\) be rational maps over
  \(S\) (i.e.\ \(a\) restricted to the generic point commutes with the structure morphisms,
  and likewise for \(b\)). The \textbf{pairing} \(a \times_S b \colon X \dashrightarrow
  Y \times_S Z\) is the rational map corresponding, under the function-field correspondence,
  to the morphism
  \[
    \operatorname{Spec} K(X)
    \xrightarrow{\;\langle a_\eta,\, b_\eta \rangle\;}
    Y \times_S Z
  \]
  obtained from the generic-point morphisms \(a_\eta \colon \operatorname{Spec} K(X) \to Y\)
  and \(b_\eta \colon \operatorname{Spec} K(X) \to Z\) by the universal property of the fibre
  product (their composites with the structure morphisms both equal
  \(\operatorname{Spec} K(X) \to \operatorname{Spec}\mathcal O_{X,\eta} \to X \to S\)).
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
  \label{lem:rationalMap_prod_proj}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.prod_compHom_fst}
  \uses{def:rationalMap_prod}
  The two projections of the pairing recover the factors, and the pairing is again over
  \(S\):
  \[
    (a \times_S b) \circ \mathrm{pr}_1 = a,
    \qquad
    (a \times_S b) \circ \mathrm{pr}_2 = b,
    \qquad
    (a \times_S b) \circ \bigl(\mathrm{pr}_1 \circ s_Y\bigr) = s_X,
  \]
  where \(\mathrm{pr}_1 \colon Y \times_S Z \to Y\), \(\mathrm{pr}_2 \colon Y \times_S Z \to Z\)
  are the projections (the last equation being the statement that \(a \times_S b\) is over
  \(S\)).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}\leanok
  Right-composition with a morphism is functorial on generic-point morphisms:
  \((\varphi \circ g)_\eta = \varphi_\eta \circ g\) for any rational map \(\varphi\) and
  morphism \(g\). Hence the generic-point morphism of \((a \times_S b) \circ \mathrm{pr}_1\) is
  \(\langle a_\eta, b_\eta\rangle \circ \mathrm{pr}_1 = a_\eta\), which is the generic-point
  morphism of \(a\); since a rational map out of an integral scheme is determined by its
  generic-point morphism, \((a \times_S b) \circ \mathrm{pr}_1 = a\). The second identity is
  symmetric. The over-\(S\) identity follows by combining the first identity with
  associativity of right-composition, \((a \times_S b) \circ (\mathrm{pr}_1 \circ s_Y) =
  \bigl((a \times_S b) \circ \mathrm{pr}_1\bigr) \circ s_Y = a \circ s_Y = s_X\).
\end{proof}

The first input to Milne's Theorem~3.2 is the following: a rational map from a normal
variety to a \emph{complete} variety automatically extends across every codim-\(1\)
point.

=== PartialMap.equiv def + toRationalMap_eq_iff ===
/-- Two partial maps are equivalent if they are equal on a dense open subscheme. -/
protected noncomputable
def equiv (f g : X.PartialMap Y) : Prop :=
  ∃ (W : X.Opens) (hW : Dense (W : Set X)) (hWl : W ≤ f.domain) (hWr : W ≤ g.domain),

/-- Precomposing the rational map of a *morphism* `g : X ⟶ Y` with an open morphism
`p : W ⟶ X` is the rational map of the composite `p ≫ g`. This produces the over-`S`
hypotheses feeding the pairing of Milne's difference map:
`(X.hom.toRationalMap).precomp prᵢ = (prᵢ ≫ X.hom).toRationalMap`. -/
@[simp]
lemma RationalMap.precomp_hom_toRationalMap (g : X ⟶ Y) (p : W ⟶ X)
    (hp : IsOpenMap p.base) :
    (g.toRationalMap).precomp p hp = (p ≫ g).toRationalMap := by
  rw [show (g.toRationalMap : X ⤏ Y) = g.toPartialMap.toRationalMap from rfl,
    ← RationalMap.precomp_toRationalMap]
  refine congrArg PartialMap.toRationalMap (PartialMap.ext _ _ rfl ?_)
  rw [Scheme.isoOfEq_rfl, Iso.refl_hom, Category.id_comp, PartialMap.precomp_hom,
    Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap_hom, Category.assoc,
    ← Category.assoc (p ∣_ ⊤)]
